codeReview.summary: 51 adversarial stress/fuzz tests across 4 new files (1802 lines) targeting all audited subsystems from milestones 1-6. Tests cover handle table (exhaust/recycle/double-close/use-after-close/type-confusion), channel state machine (signal storms/close interleaving/closed_count saturation), EEVDF scheduler (extreme vruntime/weight/deadline overflow/fuzzing), futex (hash collision/rapid wait-wake/wake_all/wake-zero), timer (deadline overflow/saturation/counter_to_ns), process (page_count overflow/VMA end/adversarial ELF), scheduler state machine (rapid create-exit/round-robin/block-wake/deferred-drop/kill), process table (slot leak/exhaustion recovery), spinlock (rapid lock-unlock/counter wrapping), waitable registry (mass lifecycle/notify-without-waiter/double-notify), buddy allocator (rapid cycles/interleaved/scrambled/alternating/uniqueness), VMA (mass insert-lookup/address-limit/reverse-scrambled/permissions/boundary), and slab (size_class fuzz/power-of-two/above-max/alignment). Tests that import actual kernel code via #[path] (handle.rs, scheduling_algorithm.rs, page_allocator.rs, memory_region.rs, slab.rs) correctly follow the existing test architecture pattern. Tests for subsystems where #[path] is incompatible (channel, futex, timer helpers, scheduler state machine, process table, spinlock, waitable) faithfully duplicate pure logic models — this is consistent with the documented constraint that kernel targets aarch64-unknown-none. Code is clean, well-structured with section headers mapping to milestone findings, and well-commented. All 631 tests pass, kernel builds, stress-test passes.

issues.description: Several test sections (channel, futex, timer, scheduler state machine, process table, waitable) test local model implementations rather than actual kernel code. While this is a known architectural limitation (kernel targets aarch64-unknown-none) and is consistent with AGENTS.md guidance, it means these tests validate behavioral contracts rather than exercising the actual kernel code paths. The most critical modules (handle, scheduling_algorithm) ARE tested against real kernel code via #[path]. If the kernel implementation drifts from these models, the tests won't catch it. Consider adding comments at each model section referencing the exact kernel source file and function being modeled, so future maintainers can verify model fidelity.

The futex hash collision test filters addresses where bucket_index == 0 to generate collisions. This is correct but the local BUCKET_COUNT=64 and hash function (pa >> 2) % 64 must match the kernel's actual implementation. If the kernel changes its bucket count or hash function, this test silently becomes non-adversarial. A comment noting the kernel's actual hash function location would help.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's skillFeedback suggests documenting that #[path] includes don't work inside nested mod blocks, requiring tests needing kernel includes to be separate files. This IS partially documented in AGENTS.md ('tests must duplicate/stub pure logic') but the specific technical reason (#[path] + nested mod incompatibility) is not in the kernel-auditor skill's 'Test Architecture' or 'Special Cases' sections. Adding a note like 'When using #[path] to include kernel modules, each must be a top-level mod in a separate integration test file — #[path] does not compose with inline mod blocks' would prevent future workers from wasting time discovering this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The kernel-auditor skill could mention that #[path] includes don't work inside nested mod blocks - tests needing kernel includes should be separate files.' Worker correctly separated adversarial_buddy.rs, adversarial_vma.rs, adversarial_slab.rs as standalone files for this reason.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description specifies 'stress-test.sh 30' but the worker used 45s because the stress test takes ~32s to complete. services.yaml already uses 45s. The feature description's timeout value appears stale/incorrect. Worker documented this as a discovered issue.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues: 'Pre-existing: stress-test.sh 30 times out because the stress test needs ~32s to complete. Passes with timeout >= 35s.' services.yaml: 'stress: cd system && ./stress-test.sh 45'

summary: Pass. Worker delivered 51 well-crafted adversarial stress/fuzz tests across 4 files covering all audited subsystems from milestones 1-6. Tests systematically exercise boundary values (u64::MAX, u32::MAX, zero), rapid cycling (100K+ iterations), resource exhaustion/recovery, and hash collision patterns. Five kernel modules are tested against actual code via #[path]; remaining subsystems use faithful behavioral models per project test architecture constraints. Code is clean, well-organized with clear section headers mapping to specific milestone findings, and follows all project conventions. Two non-blocking observations: (1) model-based tests could include references to the kernel source they model for maintainability, (2) futex hash parameters should document their kernel source. All verification steps pass: 631 tests green, kernel builds, stress-test passes.
